title: The 'is this a bridge infra teardown?' predicate (errors.Is ErrArtifactTimeout || errors.Is ErrTransientBridgeFailure) is now hand-spelled in ~6-7 sites; the new core.IsInfraTeardownError should be the single source, with the inline spellings that mean the SAME concept adopting it
summary: The quota-false-FAIL fix (commit landing 2026-07-15, reconcile-transient-terminal-error) introduced core.IsInfraTeardownError(err) = errors.Is(err, ErrArtifactTimeout) || errors.Is(err, ErrTransientBridgeFailure) as the single-source reconcile trigger in the phase runner. BOTH reviewers (go-reviewer + code-simplifier) independently flagged that the same predicate — or a component of it — is already hand-spelled at multiple call sites, so the codebase now has a 7th spelling of the concept. This violates the standing never_duplicate_centralize_via_design_patterns rule and creates DRIFT RISK: if a THIRD infra-teardown sentinel is ever added, an implementer must find and update every hand-spelled site or the definitions silently diverge.
root_cause: IsInfraTeardownError was added as a NEW exported helper without retrofitting the existing inline spellings, because the fix was deliberately kept surgical/integrity-kernel-scoped (touching core dispatch/retry paths would have broadened the blast radius of a hot quota fix). Deferred to this follow-up so the consolidation is its own audited change.
fix: Adopt core.IsInfraTeardownError at the sites that mean the SAME concept (timeout OR transient), MOST NOTABLY orchestrator.go:~59 optionalInfraSkip which is exactly !errors.Is(err, ErrArtifactTimeout) && !isTransientBridgeError(err) == !IsInfraTeardownError(err). CAUTION — do NOT blind-replace: some sites are a COMPONENT, not the full predicate (retry_backoff.go:~55 isTransientBridgeError is transient-ONLY and must stay; sites that check ErrArtifactTimeout-only or ErrTransientBridgeFailure-only are distinct predicates and must NOT collapse to IsInfraTeardownError). For each candidate (orchestrator.go, cyclerun_dispatch.go:~197,242, evaluate_batch.go:~99, failure_hook.go:~70, failure_learning.go:~215) confirm the exact semantics FIRST, then replace only the true (timeout OR transient) spellings. Keep isTransientBridgeError as the reusable transient-only component.
acceptance: Each replaced site's before/after behavior is proven identical by its EXISTING test staying green (this is a behavior-preserving refactor, not a fix — no verdict changes)
A grep-style assertion (or ADR note) documents that (timeout OR transient) is spelled exactly ONCE via IsInfraTeardownError; transient-only via isTransientBridgeError
go vet + apicover -enforce green on all touched packages; -race -count=3 stable across internal/core + internal/phases/runner
NO site that is timeout-only or transient-only was incorrectly widened to the union predicate (call out each in the build report with its confirmed semantics)
connects_to: reconcile-transient-terminal-error (the quota-false-FAIL fix that introduced IsInfraTeardownError — this is its DRY-consolidation follow-up)
operator_note: Queued 2026-07-15 from the cycle-835 quota-fix review. Both reviewers (go-reviewer WARNING-nit + code-simplifier reported-not-edited) converged on this exact finding. Weight 0.7: real maintainability/drift risk on the integrity kernel, but zero active correctness bug (all sites are individually correct + tested today). Implementer MUST verify per-site semantics before replacing — this item's whole risk is a blind widen of a timeout-only or transient-only site into the union.
